narration: Uh-Huh Runtime is a governance prototype for AI agents before they take consequential actions. This first version focuses on one control gap: recovery ownership.

Before an agent deploys to production, Uh-Huh checks whether someone owns rollback and support coverage if the deploy fails. In the first scenario, the agent proposes a production deploy with no owners or rollback plan.
Uh-Huh does not produce a vague risk score. It detects a specific control gap and asks the minimum useful question. We answer that question by supplying an owner and rollback plan.
With the control gap resolved, Uh-Huh allows the action. If the action already includes recovery ownership evidence, Uh-Huh allows it immediately.
Every decision writes an audit record: the action, detected gap, missing evidence, question, resolution evidence, and final decision.
That is the core proof of V0.1: control gap detected, minimum useful question asked, evidence supplied, action allowed.
